title: Generics, Constraints, Inference, and Variance · Spiral Review
summary: Build reusable selectors and repositories that preserve relationships between keys, inputs, outputs, and callbacks. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a relationship-preserving generic data toolkit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Introduce a type parameter only when multiple positions share a relationship that callers need preserved.
Constrain operations while returning the caller-specific subtype rather than widening to the constraint.
Use keyof and indexed access types to connect property names with their corresponding value types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Generic Relationships · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — A lookup accepts keys "id" and "status" from records numbered 6–8. Preserve the relationship between the selected key and return type, then reject a callback that handles a narrower record than callers may supply.

Which action demonstrates Generic Relationships without dropping earlier contracts?

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze covariance, contravariance, invariance, and bivariant compatibility from producer and consumer positions. Artifact under review: a relationship-preserving generic data toolkit.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
